assumptions: The issue concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9.
The intragroup dividends and any related receivable are denominated in a foreign currency.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities and components of them that expose the entity to changes in fair value affecting profit or loss.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for variability in cash flows of recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions, including certain intragroup foreign currency exposures in consolidation.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk recognised in other comprehensive income.
